name: ViewModel State Preservation
description: Retain UI state across configuration changes using lifecycle-aware ViewModel.
when_to_use: Android: always. iOS: for complex screens. Any screen with async loading.
trade_offs: ViewModel survives rotation but not process death; use SavedStateHandle for persistence.
implementation_notes: Android: ViewModel + SavedStateHandle. iOS: ObservableObject retained by View. Compose: rememberSaveable.
keywords: viewmodel state configuration change lifecycle savedstatehandle

name: StateFlow / State Observation
description: Emit UI state as an observable stream from ViewModel to View.
when_to_use: When UI needs to reactively update based on data or user action changes.
trade_offs: Cold vs hot stream semantics matter; StateFlow replays last value.
implementation_notes: Android: StateFlow + collectAsState(). iOS: @Published + Combine. KMM: StateFlow with native wrapper.
keywords: stateflow livedata published combine observe reactive

name: Side Effects / One-Shot Events
description: Non-state events that should be consumed exactly once (navigation; snackbar; error toast).
when_to_use: When an action triggers a transient effect that should not replay on recomposition.
trade_offs: SharedFlow vs Channel debate; avoid putting events in StateFlow (replays on rotation).
implementation_notes: Android: Channel or SharedFlow(replay=0). iOS: PassthroughSubject. Compose: LaunchedEffect.
keywords: side effect event channel sharedflow passthrough oneshot

name: Paging / Infinite Scroll State
description: Manage paginated data loading with load states (loading; error; empty; loaded).
when_to_use: When displaying server-paginated content (feeds; search results; catalogs).
trade_offs: Complex error recovery per page; placeholder item management.
implementation_notes: Android: Paging 3 library. iOS: custom with AsyncSequence. Compose: LazyPagingItems. Flutter: infinite_scroll_pagination.
keywords: paging pagination infinite scroll load more cursor offset

name: Form State Management
description: Track field values; validation errors; dirty/pristine state; and submission status.
when_to_use: When collecting multi-field user input with real-time validation.
trade_offs: Must handle focus changes; keyboard actions; and field interdependencies.
implementation_notes: Android: custom state class or form libraries. iOS: @FocusState + validation. Compose: state per field or form state holder.
keywords: form state validation field dirty pristine submit

name: Loading / Error / Success Pattern
description: Sealed class or enum representing async operation states.
when_to_use: When any screen loads data from network or database asynchronously.
trade_offs: Boilerplate for simple screens; consider a generic Result wrapper.
implementation_notes: Android: sealed class UiState<T> { Loading; Success(data); Error(message) }. iOS: enum LoadingState. KMM: shared.
keywords: loading error success sealed class uistate async result

name: Process Death Restoration
description: Restore state after Android kills the process in the background.
when_to_use: Android: always consider for critical user input screens. iOS: not applicable (different lifecycle).
trade_offs: SavedStateHandle has size limits; persist large data to DB instead.
implementation_notes: Android: SavedStateHandle + rememberSaveable. Bundle limit ~500KB. For complex state; use Room cache.
keywords: process death savedstatehandle bundle restore persist

name: Shared State Between Screens
description: State that multiple screens need to read/write (cart; auth; user profile).
when_to_use: When the same data is displayed or modified across multiple navigation destinations.
trade_offs: Avoid global mutable state; use scoped ViewModels or DI-provided state holders.
implementation_notes: Android: activityViewModels() or shared DI scope. iOS: @EnvironmentObject. Compose: shared ViewModel scope.
keywords: shared state global cross screen activity environment

name: Undo / Redo State Stack
description: Maintain a stack of previous states to allow reversing user actions.
when_to_use: When user performs destructive or creative actions (text editing; drawing; reordering).
trade_offs: Memory grows with history depth; must define a reasonable max stack size.
implementation_notes: Implement command pattern or state snapshot list. Limit to 20-50 entries.
keywords: undo redo history stack command pattern state

name: Offline Queue & Sync State
description: Queue mutations while offline and synchronize when connectivity returns.
when_to_use: When app must support offline writes (forms; messages; field data collection).
trade_offs: Conflict resolution is complex; must handle partial sync failures.
implementation_notes: Android: WorkManager + Room queue. iOS: BackgroundTasks + CoreData. KMM: SQLDelight + ktor retry.
keywords: offline sync queue workmanager background conflict

name: Cache Invalidation & Refresh
description: Strategy for invalidating stale cached data and triggering fresh loads.
when_to_use: When cached data has a TTL or can be invalidated by server-side changes.
trade_offs: Aggressive invalidation wastes bandwidth; lazy invalidation shows stale data.
implementation_notes: timestamp-based TTL; server push (WebSocket/FCM); manual pull-to-refresh; ETag/Last-Modified headers.
keywords: cache invalidation refresh ttl etag stale data

name: Shared State in KMM
description: Share ViewModel or state holder logic across Android and iOS via Kotlin Multiplatform.
when_to_use: When using KMM and want consistent state management across platforms.
trade_offs: iOS needs wrapper around Kotlin StateFlow; lifecycle management differs.
implementation_notes: KMM-ViewModel library or custom expect/actual. SKIE for Swift-friendly Flow.
keywords: kmm shared viewmodel state multiplatform skie flow
